---
title: "Equilibrium Separation Stages Reference Page<br/>*The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong?*"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---

{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}

# Intro to *The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong?*

Many industrial separation processes are modeled as a series of **equilibrium stages**. In each stage the entering streams mix, reach thermodynamic equilibrium, and then separate into outgoing phases.

This model forms the basis for analyzing processes such as **distillation columns, flash separators, and liquid–liquid extraction units**. The videos on this page introduce the equilibrium-stage concept and demonstrate how it is used in separation calculations.

---

## Part 1

This video introduces the equilibrium-stage model and explains the assumptions used when modeling separation processes such as distillation columns.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/bY9fl5DJzmk?si=brE9cHtUxJxGpBno" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](85-EquilibriumSeparationStages1Visuals.qmd)

---

## Part 2

This video demonstrates equilibrium-stage calculations for multicomponent mixtures using **Raoult’s Law**.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/wc6gv7lNtI0?si=NNrEaQFVhE5Cmw1t" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](86-EquilibriumSeparationStages2Visuals.qmd)

---

## Part 3

This video applies the equilibrium-stage concept to **liquid–liquid extraction**, demonstrating how partially miscible liquids can be separated using stagewise contact.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/jcChVoELvFo?si=PusFY_zjmwddEeSb" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](86.5-EquilibriumSeparationStages3Visuals.qmd)

---

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Hexane, Heptane, Octane Equilibrium Stage Example](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/HexHeptOctEqStage.ods) – A spreadsheet with the three-component Raoult's law calculation from Part 2
- [Liquid-Liquid Extraction Example](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/Example3LiqLiqExtact.ods) – A spreadsheet with the liquid-liquid extraction calculation from Part 3

---

## Definitions

Equilibrium Separation Stage  
: A theoretical stage in which incoming streams are assumed to mix completely and then separate into outgoing phases that are **in thermodynamic equilibrium with each other**.

Phase Fraction  
: The fraction of the total mixture that exists as a given phase. Phase fractions can be expressed on either a **mole basis** or a **mass basis**, which generally give different numerical values.

Liquid Fraction  
: The fraction of the total mixture present as liquid. It may be defined using either mass fractions or mole fractions.

Vapor Fraction  
: The fraction of the total mixture present as vapor. It may be defined using either mass fractions or mole fractions.

Distillation  
: A separation process that uses repeated **vaporization and condensation** to separate components based on differences in volatility.

Liquid–Liquid Extraction  
: A separation process in which a solute is transferred from one liquid phase to another by contacting **partially miscible liquids** and allowing them to separate.

---

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### Two videos back

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 2**  
*BUBL T, DEW T*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/9GyrfJzg-tg), [Info Page](../../balances/material/RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/75-Visuals.qmd)

### Previous video

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 3**  
*FLASH*, *Pxy*, *Txy*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/bG15qQ9gB4c), [Info Page](../../balances/material/RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/75.5-Visuals.qmd)

### Next video

**Using DWSIM to Create Binary Phase Diagrams**  
*DWSIM, Binary Phase Diagrams*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/5NUchIg8q7E), [Info Page](../../balances/material/DWSIMBinaryPhaseVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

### Two videos forward

**Just Try to Break This Law! I Dare You!**  
*Intro to 1st Law*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/ZHWT9Jw7fzY), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/Intro1stLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/89-Intro1stLawVisuals.qmd)
